tasks.description: Fix disabled-state logic in TrackLabel.tsx and compute kindIndex in TimelineEditor.tsx. In TrackLabel.tsx:201, `trackIndex === 0` must become `kindIndex === 0` (the track's position within its kind group). Similarly line 214, `trackIndex >= trackCount - 1` must become `kindIndex >= sameKindCount - 1`. Add a new `kindIndex` prop to TrackLabelProps. In TimelineEditor.tsx:217-234, compute kindIndex per track by counting preceding tracks of the same kind: `data.tracks.slice(0, index).filter(t => t.kind === track.kind).length`. Pass as prop to TrackLabel.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified by code inspection and targeted lint: `TrackLabel` now disables the up/down controls using `kindIndex` and `sameKindCount`, and `TimelineEditor` computes `kindIndex` from preceding same-kind tracks. This fixes the mixed-kind boundary case where the first audio track was incorrectly treated as movable upward based on global index.

tasks.description: Fix handleReorderTrack in useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:286-308 to find the correct same-kind swap target. Current code computes `targetIndex = index + direction` and bails if kinds differ. Instead, scan in `direction` from `index` to find the next track with the same kind. If none found, return early. The swap logic (lines 305-306) stays the same once the correct targetIndex is found.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified by code inspection and targeted lint: `handleReorderTrack` now scans in the requested direction until it finds the next track of the same kind before swapping. Mixed-kind boundaries now no-op when no same-kind target exists, while same-kind neighbors still reorder normally.

tasks.description: Add vitest tests for same-kind boundary reorder logic. Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` (or a pure function test if the logic is extractable). Test cases: (1) [V1,V2,A1,A2] — moving V2 down is no-op, moving A1 up is no-op, moving A1 down swaps with A2, moving V1 down swaps with V2. (2) [V1,A1] — all moves are no-ops (sameKindCount=1). (3) Single kind [V1,V2,V3] — normal reorder works. Follow existing pattern in serialize.test.ts. If handleReorderTrack is hard to unit-test due to hook dependencies, extract the pure reorder logic into a standalone function in timeline-data.ts or a utility, then test that.
tasks.executor_notes: Extracted the arrow-button reorder logic into the pure helper `reorderTracksByDirection` and added a focused vitest suite that covers mixed kinds, single-of-each-kind, and single-kind-only configurations. Verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed with 3/3 tests.

tasks.description: Add nested DndContext and two SortableContext groups in TimelineEditor.tsx. Wrap the track label list (lines 217-234) in a new DndContext with its own sensors: PointerSensor (activationConstraint: { distance: 5 }) AND KeyboardSensor (with sortableKeyboardCoordinates) — both are required per FLAG-001. Inside, split tracks into two SortableContext groups by kind: one for visual tracks, one for audio tracks. Each SortableContext uses verticalListSortingStrategy and gets IDs like `track-${track.id}`. Add an onDragEnd handler that extracts active.id/over.id, validates same kind, and calls a new handleMoveTrack function. Import DndContext, SortableContext, verticalListSortingStrategy, closestCenter, useSensors, useSensor, PointerSensor, KeyboardSensor, sortableKeyboardCoordinates from @dnd-kit packages.
tasks.executor_notes: TimelineEditor now wraps the track label list in a nested dnd-kit `DndContext`, uses distinct `PointerSensor` and `KeyboardSensor` instances with `sortableKeyboardCoordinates`, and renders separate visual/audio `SortableContext` groups. Drag-end handling validates same-kind moves before calling `handleMoveTrack`. Verification: targeted eslint passed on the touched TimelineEditor and hook/state files.

tasks.description: Convert TrackLabel to a sortable item. Add useSortable hook (id: `track-${track.id}`) following SortableClip.tsx:86-96 pattern. Apply transform/transition styles and isDragging opacity (0.5) to the root div. Replace the ArrowUp/ArrowDown buttons (lines 196-221) with a single GripVertical drag handle that receives {...attributes, ...listeners} from useSortable. Keep the handle in the hover overlay area (inside the div at line 99). Remove onReorder prop, ArrowUp, ArrowDown imports. Add GripVertical import from lucide-react. Update TrackLabelProps: remove onReorder, add a sortable `id` prop (string).
tasks.executor_notes: TrackLabel now uses `useSortable` with the sortable id passed from TimelineEditor, applies transform/transition styles plus `isDragging` opacity, and shows a single `GripVertical` handle in the existing hover overlay. The old ArrowUp/ArrowDown controls, `onReorder`, and the stale same-kind props/imports were removed. Verification: targeted eslint passed on the updated TrackLabel/TimelineEditor flow.

tasks.description: Add handleMoveTrack callback in useTimelineTrackManagement.ts and export it. Signature: `handleMoveTrack(activeId: string, overId: string)`. Implementation: find both tracks in resolvedConfig.tracks, validate same kind, use arrayMove from @dnd-kit/sortable to reorder the tracks array, call applyResolvedConfigEdit. Remove the old handleReorderTrack export since it's no longer used by TrackLabel. Update the hook's return value accordingly. Update TimelineEditor.tsx to use handleMoveTrack instead of handleReorderTrack.
tasks.executor_notes: `handleMoveTrack(activeId, overId)` is the only exported track-reorder callback in the video-editor hook surface now. It reorders through `moveTrackWithinKind`, which validates same-kind moves before calling `arrayMove`, and all `handleReorderTrack` references were removed from the hook return value, state/context types, and TimelineEditor usage. Verification: targeted eslint passed and the existing reorder vitest file still passed.

tasks.description: Update tests from T3 to also cover handleMoveTrack / arrayMove-based reordering. Add test cases: (1) moving a track to a new position within its kind group produces correct order, (2) drag between different kinds is rejected, (3) verify the visual-before-audio invariant is preserved after reorder + save/load round-trip (serialize then buildTimelineData and check track order).
tasks.executor_notes: Extended the existing track-management vitest file to cover the arrayMove-based drag reorder helper `moveTrackWithinKind`, including a successful same-kind move, a rejected cross-kind drag, and a serialize/buildTimelineData round-trip that confirms within-kind order survives load-time sorting. Verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed with 6/6 tests, and targeted eslint passed on the hook/test files.
commands_run: rg -n "moveTrackWithinKind|serializeForDisk|buildTimelineData|preserves within-kind order|rejects drag moves|moves a track to a new position" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts

tasks.description: Visual polish and final validation. Add isDragging opacity (already in T5). Verify track list scroll sync (trackListRef onScroll → timelineRef.setScrollTop) still works during/after drag. Verify no interference with clip DnD (the app-level DndContext in App.tsx:58 should not receive track drag events since they're in a nested DndContext). Verify no interference with external drop (onDragOver/onDragLeave/onDrop on timelineWrapperRef at line 239-241). Run `npx vitest run` to ensure all tests pass. Do a visual check that the GripVertical handle looks correct in the hover overlay.
tasks.executor_notes: No additional code changes were required. Verification by code inspection confirms: `TrackLabel` already applies `isDragging` opacity and keeps the grip handle in the hover overlay; the track list still syncs scroll position via `trackListRef`'s `onScroll` handler; the nested track `DndContext` remains scoped to the track-list column while the app-level DnD handlers stay on the outer app shell; and the external file-drop handlers remain attached to `timelineWrapperRef`, outside the nested track DnD subtree. `npx vitest run` was executed but does not pass repo-wide because of numerous unrelated existing failures outside the video-editor track-reorder area.

watch_items: [DEBT] track-reorder-accessibility: FLAG-001 — must add KeyboardSensor alongside PointerSensor in the nested DndContext (T4). Do NOT ship pointer-only.
[DEBT] track-reorder-testing: FLAG-003 — T3 and T7 address this. Ensure tests actually cover the same-kind boundary edge cases, not just happy paths.
FLAG-002 (minor): nested DndContext may be over-engineering since timeline clip dragging doesn't use dnd-kit. However the nested approach is safer and low-cost, so proceed as planned.
sortTracksByKind (timeline-data.ts:320-324) enforces visual-before-audio on load. Reorder logic must never allow cross-kind moves. This is the key invariant.

App-level DndContext (App.tsx:58) handles shot/gallery DnD with DragOverlay. The nested track DndContext must not leak events up. Verify by testing that dragging a track handle does NOT trigger the app-level onDragStart.
The track list scrolls in sync with the timeline canvas (TimelineEditor.tsx:213-215). Drag operations must not break this scroll sync.
External drop zone (onDragOver/onDrop on timelineWrapperRef) must continue to work for file drops from outside the app.
Do not regress any debt watch items. This change touches only track labels and reorder logic — no impact on agent chat, bulk panels, context menus, task creation, or realtime subscriptions.

sense_checks.question: Does the up button correctly disable for the first track of each kind group (e.g., first audio track at global index 2 in [V1,V2,A1,A2])?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `TrackLabel` now checks `kindIndex === 0`, and `TimelineEditor` computes `kindIndex` by counting preceding same-kind tracks. In `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, `A1` has global index 2 but `kindIndex` 0, so the up button disables correctly.

sense_checks.question: In a [V1,V2,A1,A2] config, does moving V2 down correctly no-op (not swap with A1), and does moving A1 down correctly swap with A2?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The reorder handler now scans for the next same-kind track before swapping. In `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, moving `V2` down finds no later visual track and no-ops; moving `A1` down finds `A2` and swaps.

sense_checks.question: Do tests cover all three configurations: mixed kinds, single-of-each-kind, and single-kind-only? Is the reorder logic testable as a pure function?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The new test file covers all three required configurations: mixed kinds `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, single-of-each-kind `[V1,A1]`, and single-kind-only `[V1,V2,V3]`. The reorder logic is now directly testable as the pure helper `reorderTracksByDirection`.

sense_checks.question: Are there two separate SortableContext groups (one per kind), and does the nested DndContext include both PointerSensor AND KeyboardSensor?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. TimelineEditor now contains two separate `SortableContext` groups, one for visual tracks and one for audio tracks, inside a nested `DndContext` configured with both `PointerSensor` and `KeyboardSensor` using `sortableKeyboardCoordinates`.

sense_checks.question: Is the GripVertical handle visible on hover (matching existing button UX), and are the old ArrowUp/ArrowDown buttons fully removed with no dead imports?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `TrackLabel` now renders a `GripVertical` handle inside the existing hover overlay, and the old ArrowUp/ArrowDown buttons plus their related props/imports are removed. Targeted eslint passed on the updated component wiring.

sense_checks.question: Does handleMoveTrack validate same-kind before calling arrayMove, and is the old handleReorderTrack fully removed from the hook's return value?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `handleMoveTrack` reorders through `moveTrackWithinKind`, which checks that both tracks share the same kind before calling `arrayMove`, and `handleReorderTrack` has been fully removed from the video-editor hook/context surface.

sense_checks.question: Do round-trip tests (reorder → serialize → buildTimelineData) confirm that sortTracksByKind preserves the user's within-kind ordering?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The new round-trip test reorders tracks with `moveTrackWithinKind`, serializes the reordered config with `serializeForDisk`, rebuilds it with `buildTimelineData`, and verifies the rebuilt track order remains `['V2', 'V1', 'A1', 'A2']`, confirming that `sortTracksByKind` preserves the user's within-kind ordering.

sense_checks.question: Does dragging a track handle avoid triggering the app-level DndContext handlers, and does the track list scroll sync still work after a drag completes?
sense_checks.executor_note: By code inspection, yes: the track list still calls `timelineRef.current?.setScrollTop(...)` from `trackListRef`'s `onScroll`, and track dragging is contained within the nested `DndContext` mounted inside the track-list column while the app-level DnD handlers remain on the outer app shell. The external file-drop handlers also remain attached to `timelineWrapperRef`, outside the nested track DnD subtree. I could not perform an interactive browser drag session in this environment, so this validation is static plus test-run based rather than a live UI interaction.

meta_commentary:
**Execution guidance:**

**Phase 1 (T1-T3): Bug fixes.** T1 and T2 are independent and can be done in parallel. The root cause is clear from code inspection: TrackLabel.tsx:201 uses `trackIndex === 0` (global) which is never true for audio tracks, and handleReorderTrack:296-303 uses `index + direction` which hits the kind boundary and bails. Both fixes are small. T3 depends on T2 — extract the reorder logic into a pure function if possible so it's easy to test. The existing serialize.test.ts provides the test infrastructure pattern.

**Phase 2 (T4-T8): Drag-to-reorder.** T4 (SortableContext setup) must come first since T5 (TrackLabel conversion) and T6 (handleMoveTrack) both depend on the DndContext being in place. T5 and T6 can be done in parallel. T7 extends T3's tests. T8 is final integration validation.

**Critical gotcha — nested DndContext:** The app-level DndContext in App.tsx wraps the entire app. A nested DndContext for track reordering will intercept drag events before they bubble to the app level — this is the desired behavior. However, verify that `closestCenter` collision detection in the nested context doesn't interfere. The nested context should have its own sensors instance (don't reuse the app-level one).

**Critical gotcha — two SortableContext groups rendering:** The track list currently renders all tracks in a single `.map()` loop. With two SortableContext groups, you'll need to either: (a) render two separate loops (one for visual, one for audio) which changes the DOM structure, or (b) use a single loop but wrap the items in their respective SortableContext via a parent component. Option (a) is simpler but may need a visual separator between groups. Choose whichever maintains the existing DOM structure closest.

**Keyboard accessibility (FLAG-001):** Non-negotiable. The existing arrow buttons are keyboard-focusable. @dnd-kit's KeyboardSensor + sortableKeyboardCoordinates provides keyboard drag support. Import from `@dnd-kit/sortable`. This is already wired in App.tsx:51-53 as a reference pattern.

**File inventory:** TrackLabel.tsx (full rewrite of action buttons area), TimelineEditor.tsx (add DndContext/SortableContext wrapping + kindIndex computation), useTimelineTrackManagement.ts (fix handleReorderTrack then replace with handleMoveTrack), new test file for reorder logic.
